description: Admins have total control over the system's economic logic, user access, and final settlement structures.

features.title: Incentive Schemes (The Logic Engine)
features.usage:
Navigate to 'Schemes'. Here you define how numbers become money. You can create four types of formulas:
1. **Percentage**: Straight % of sale. Best for high-margin simple products.
2. **Tiered Bonus**: Extra % after crossing a target. Drives volume.
3. **Qty Threshold**: Flat bonus after X units sold. Rewards frequency.
4. **Fixed Per Unit**: Constant ₹ amount. Simplest model.
features.tips: Important: Schemes define the math. Staff must be 'linked' to a scheme to start earning.
features.what_not_to_do: Do not delete a scheme if it has active users; use 'Archive' or update the end date instead.

features.title: User Control & Permissions
features.usage: Review new signups in 'Approvals'. Assign them one of four roles: Admin (Governance), Manager (Team Ops), Accounts (Finance), or Salesperson (Execution).
features.tips: Assign a Manager to every Salesperson to enable hierarchical commission overrides.
features.what_not_to_do: Never share login credentials; the Audit Log tracks specific user IDs for every change.

description: Managers bridge the gap between sales and finance, ensuring accuracy and performance.

features.title: Sales Review & Dispatch
features.usage: When your team logs a sale, it enters 'Pending Review'. You must verify the client name and deal value. If correct, approve it to move it to 'Earned' status.
features.tips: Use the 'Flag' feature for disputes. This locks the commission until the conflict is resolved.
features.what_not_to_do: Do not approve sales without client verification; once 'Earned', they are ready for payment batching.

features.title: Batch Settlement Creation
features.usage: Group multiple 'Earned' items into a 'Payout Batch'. This creates a unique reference ID for the collection. Move the batch from 'Draft' to 'Submitted' for finance processing.
features.tips: Create batches weekly to keep payout timelines predictable.
features.what_not_to_do: Do not add disputed or flagged items to a batch; they will cause the entire batch to fail audit.

description: The front-line users who drive revenue and track their financial growth.

features.title: Dynamic Sales Logging
features.usage: Input Client Name, Product, and Deal Value. The system automatically fetches your active 'Scheme' and shows your estimated earnings instantly.
features.tips: Check your 'Reference ID' for every sale to track individual deals in payroll.
features.what_not_to_do: Do not log duplicate entries for the same client; use the 'Sale Date' to differentiate installments.

features.title: Performance Tracking
features.usage: Use the Dashboard to see your Monthly Target progress. The visuals show how much more you need to sell to reach the next bonus tier.
features.tips: Review your 'Rejected' logs to see manager feedback and corrective actions.
features.what_not_to_do: Avoid logging approximate deal values; commission is calculated on the exact decimal entry.

description: Responsible for final verification of settlements and disbursement of funds.

features.title: The Batch Lifecycle
features.usage: Finance accesses 'Approved' batches only. Review the 'Total Batch Amount'. Once funds are disbursed via bank transfer, mark the batch as 'Paid' in the system.
features.tips: Use the 'Payment Reference' field to link system records with bank transaction IDs.
features.what_not_to_do: Do not mark a batch as 'Paid' if individual items are still disputed.

description: Understanding the logic that keeps PayoutPower professional and audit-ready.

features.title: Reference Serial Numbers
features.usage:
Every entry has a unique ID:
1. **Sales (SL-YYYYMMDD-XXXX)**: Tracks the life of a single deal.
2. **Batches (BT-YYYYMMDD-XXXX)**: Tracks a group of settlements.
features.tips: Use these IDs in internal emails for instant traceability.
features.what_not_to_do: Do not treat the ID as just a number; it is a permanent legal identifier in the system database.
